summary: DHH describes how he suffered from seasonal allergies for years, cycling through medications and allergy vaccinations recommended by specialists, none of whom addressed the root cause: the air he was breathing. He solved the problem by installing HEPA air purifiers throughout his home, which eliminated most of his symptoms. He uses this personal experience to critique the medical profession's focus on mitigation over prevention. The broader point is that experts can overcomplicate problems that have simple environmental solutions.
key_points: Multiple allergy specialists prescribed medications and vaccinations but never asked about the air quality in his environment
HEPA air purifiers combined with air quality sensors effectively eliminated his chronic allergy symptoms for over a decade
The medical profession's focus on treating symptoms rather than addressing environmental causes represents a systemic blind spot
The dose makes the poison — reducing allergen exposure most of the time is sufficient even if you can't eliminate it entirely
Expertise can become a liability when specialists are too focused on complex solutions to see simple ones
money_quotes: But never once did an allergy doctor ask the basic question: What kind of air are you breathing?
That's what's carrying all this particulate matter, so if your idea of proper ventilation is merely to open a window, you're inviting in your nasal assailants.
That's it. That was the answer!
The difference between breathing in some allergens, some of the time, is entirely different from breathing all of it, all of the time.

Here was this entire profession of allergy specialists, and I saw at least a handful of them while I was trying to find a medical solution. None of them even thought about dealing with the environment.
Their entire field of view was restricted to dealing with mitigation rather than prevention.
Not every problem, medical or otherwise, has a simple solution. But many problems do, and you have to be careful not to be so smart that you can't see it.
key_insight: Experts can become so focused on sophisticated interventions that they overlook simple environmental fixes — sometimes the best solution is eliminating the cause rather than treating the symptoms.
